questions.prompt: Production agent observability tool for LangGraph?

choices.text: LangSmith. Auto-traces every LangGraph run; visualizes node execution; LLM calls (prompts + responses); tool invocations + results; latency + cost; errors. Setup: env vars LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2 + LANGCHAIN_API_KEY. Every agent run appears in dashboard. Production-essential for debugging + monitoring. Alternatives: custom OpenTelemetry tracing; Langfuse (open-source alternative). Print statements impractical for complex multi-step agents.

questions.explanation: LangSmith. Auto-tracing; visualization; production-essential. Or Langfuse alternative.

questions.prompt: Agent takes 30 seconds; users see blank screen meanwhile. UX fix?

choices.text: Stream intermediate steps + tokens. `async for event in app.astream(input)` yields per-node updates; `astream_events` streams LLM tokens. Show users: 1) 'Thinking...' indicator. 2) Current step ('Searching the web...'). 3) Tool results as they arrive. 4) Final answer streaming token-by-token. Perceived latency much lower; engagement maintained. Without streaming, 30 sec blank = users assume broken; with streaming, 30 sec of visible progress = engaged users. Pattern: 1) Show 'Working on it...' message. 2) Update with each node ('Searching documentation...'). 3) Stream final response. 4) Display sources/tools used. Standard pro AI UX 2026.

questions.explanation: Stream intermediate steps via astream + token streaming. Show progress. Standard pro AI UX.
